doc_richTextEditor_long_desc: RichTextEditor is a WYSIWYG (What You See Is What You Get) editor that provides a familiar word-processor-like experience for entering and formatting rich text content.
doc_richTextEditor_title: WYSIWYG editor for formatted HTML content
doc_richTextEditor_design_intent: RichTextEditor bridges the gap between plain Textarea and complex document editors. It is suited to situations where users need to structure text with headings, lists, bold/italic emphasis, or hyperlinks — without needing to write HTML themselves.
doc_richTextEditor_choice_matrix_desc: Select the appropriate component based on the expected content type and formatting needs.
doc_richTextEditor_choice_rte_when: When users need to format text (bold, italic, headings, lists, links) and the output is rendered as HTML.
doc_richTextEditor_choice_textarea_when: When only plain text is needed (comments, short descriptions) and formatting is irrelevant.
doc_richTextEditor_choice_markdown_when: When the audience is technical and Markdown syntax is preferred over visual formatting.
doc_richTextEditor_anatomy_toolbar: Toolbar: A row of formatting action buttons (bold, italic, headings, lists, link, etc.).
doc_richTextEditor_anatomy_editor: Editor Area: A contentEditable region where users type and see live-formatted text.
doc_richTextEditor_content_output: The component outputs HTML strings via the onChange callback. Sanitize the output before rendering to prevent XSS.
doc_richTextEditor_content_placeholder: Use the placeholder prop to hint at the expected content. The placeholder disappears on focus.
doc_richTextEditor_motion_toolbar: Toolbar buttons show an active (pressed) highlight when the cursor is inside formatted text, giving instant visual feedback.
doc_richTextEditor_a11y_desc: The editor area carries role='textbox' and aria-multiline. Each toolbar button has an aria-label and aria-pressed. The toolbar itself has role='toolbar'. Keyboard users can Tab into the editor and use Ctrl+B / Ctrl+I / Ctrl+U for common shortcuts.
doc_richTextEditor_scenario_article: CMS / Blog Editor: Composing articles with headings, paragraphs, and embedded links.
doc_richTextEditor_scenario_comment: Community Platforms: Rich-formatted comments or replies where bold and lists improve readability.
doc_richTextEditor_scenario_email: Email Composers: Drafting formatted emails with bullet points and hyperlinks.
doc_richTextEditor_variant_desc: RichTextEditor supports the same variant and status props as other form inputs for visual consistency.
doc_richTextEditor_toolbar_desc: The toolbar prop accepts an array of item keys and 'separator' strings, allowing full customization of which buttons appear and in what order.
doc_cascader_hover_expand_desc: By specifying `expandTrigger="hover"`, submenus can be expanded by hovering instead of clicking.
doc_cascader_long_desc: Cascader is used to navigate through a multi-level menu to drill down and select a final value.
doc_cascader_title: A component for selecting a single value by navigating through hierarchical data.
doc_checkbox_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation of the Checkbox component. Use standalone or with CheckboxGroup for multiple selections.
doc_checkbox_long_desc: Details and usage for the Checkbox component.
doc_checkbox_note_default: Generally, the default state should be unselected (off). This is crucial for user consent.
doc_checkbox_note_layout: Stack vertically and ensure a large tap area.
doc_checkbox_state_indeterminate_desc: An intermediate state used when only some child elements are selected, such as in a 'Select All' checkbox.
doc_textarea_title: Multi-line text field optimized for long-form input
doc_textarea_design_intent: Textarea is a component for enabling entry of long-form plain text such as descriptions, comments, or messages. It provides a wider entry area than a standard Input and increases the degree of freedom in writing by supporting scrolling and (where necessary) automatic resizing.
doc_textarea_choice_matrix_desc: Select according to the amount and quality of information expected to be entered.
doc_textarea_choice_textarea_when: When entry of sentences of several lines or more is anticipated (e.g., contact details).
doc_textarea_choice_input_when: When entering short, single pieces of information such as names, addresses (partial), or phone numbers.
doc_textarea_anatomy_field: Text Area: An editable area that accepts multiple lines of text.
doc_textarea_anatomy_resize: Resize Handle: A part that allows users to manually adjust the height (browser function or style setting).
doc_textarea_content_prefill: If the entry content is unclear, present concrete description examples as placeholders or help text.
doc_textarea_motion_expand: If there is an auto-size (automatic resizing) function, height changes smoothly with entry, eliminating inconvenience during writing.
doc_textarea_a11y_desc: When entering long sentences, clarify the focus state so that users do not lose sight of their entry position, and communicate entry limits etc. with aria-describedby as needed.
doc_textarea_scenario_comment: SNS Posting: Entering long comments for photos or videos.
doc_textarea_scenario_contact: Contact Us: Sending question content in a free-form description format.
doc_checkbox_design_intent: Checkboxes are used to allow users to select multiple items from a list, or to toggle individual options (such as consent) on or off. The goal is to provide a clear indication of selection.
doc_checkbox_choice_matrix_desc: Choose the most appropriate component based on the number and nature of choices.
doc_checkbox_choice_check_when: When multiple answers are possible, or for a single consent item.
doc_checkbox_choice_radio_when: When exactly one option must be selected from multiple choices.
doc_checkbox_choice_switch_when: When toggling a feature on or off results in an immediate change.
doc_checkbox_anatomy_input: Input: A hidden element that holds the actual checked state.
doc_checkbox_anatomy_box: Box: A custom visual part that represents the checked state.
doc_checkbox_anatomy_label: Label: A clickable text area that enhances affordance.
doc_checkbox_content_positive: Use positive phrasing for labels (e.g., "Enable Notifications").
doc_checkbox_content_no_period: Avoid putting a period at the end of the labels.
doc_checkbox_motion_check: Apply an animation where the checkmark appears to be drawn, providing a sense of achievement.
doc_checkbox_a11y_keyboard: Toggle the checked state using the Space key.
doc_checkbox_a11y_label: Explicitly link label text with the input so it can be announced by screen readers.
doc_checkbox_scenario_agreement: Terms of Service Consent: Placed as a required item before form submission.
doc_checkbox_scenario_filter: Search Filters: Selecting multiple categories to narrow down results.
doc_checkbox_scenario_newsletter: Notification Settings: Selecting multiple categories of information to receive in user profiles or settings.
doc_checkbox_when_to_use: Best for selecting multiple options or for Yes/No confirmations (e.g., terms of service consent).
doc_checkboxgroup_long_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Checkbox Group component.
doc_colorinput_long_desc: A component that provides a color input.
doc_colorinput_title: Field for selecting colors and entering hex codes with preview
doc_colorpicker_long_desc: A component for users to pick colors. It wraps the native input type="color" and adjusts its styles.
doc_colorpicker_title: Component for choosing colors from a visual palette
doc_combobox_long_desc: A combobox is an input component that allows the user to select options from a list or filter by typing text.
doc_combobox_title: Select box with search and autocomplete functionality
doc_combobox_design_intent: Combobox is a component that combines the advantages of traditional Selectbox (selection) and Input (free-form entry). It provides high flexibility, such as performing filtering searches from a vast number of choices and allowing entry of new values not in the list as needed.
doc_combobox_choice_matrix_desc: Select according to the number of choices and the need for free-form entry.
doc_combobox_choice_combobox_when: When there are a very large number of choices (100 or more), or when you want to allow custom values not in the list to be entered.
doc_combobox_choice_selectbox_when: When you want to restrict selection to only predefined lists.
doc_combobox_anatomy_field: Text Field: The area for entering search queries or free description.
doc_combobox_anatomy_list: Suggestion List: A menu that displays candidates filtered based on the entered content.
doc_combobox_content_no_results: Display appropriate messages such as 'Not found' when there are no search results, suggesting the next action (e.g., create new).
doc_combobox_motion_filter: The list is filtered smoothly with each input, visually communicating the narrowing down of results.
doc_combobox_a11y_autocomplete: Properly set aria-autocomplete to communicate to screen readers that the list changes with input.
doc_combobox_scenario_tag: Tag Management: Selecting from existing tags or creating and entering new ones.
doc_combobox_scenario_address: Address Entry: Completion for prefectures or municipalities.
doc_datepicker_a11y_2: `aria-label` or `aria-describedby` is set for the input field.
doc_datepicker_a11y_4: Disabled dates are explicitly marked with aria-disabled.
doc_datepicker_adv_1: Intuitive date entry without needing specific calendar knowledge.
doc_datepicker_adv_2: Visual confirmation of dates via the calendar UI.
doc_datepicker_adv_3: Automatically prevents format errors during entry.
doc_datepicker_adv_4: Full support for accessible keyboard navigation.
doc_datepicker_clear_btn_desc: Optionally display a button to instantly clear the selected date.
doc_datepicker_control_types_desc: DatePicker supports both controlled and uncontrolled patterns.
doc_datepicker_custom_format_desc: Control the display format in the input field using the format property.
doc_datepicker_custom_popup_desc: Customize the content of the calendar popup using available slots.
doc_datepicker_flow_1: Click the first time to select the start date.
doc_datepicker_flow_2: Click the second time to select the end date.
doc_datepicker_flow_3: The range between the start and end dates is highlighted during selection.
doc_datepicker_flow_4: If a date earlier than the start date is selected as the end date, they are automatically swapped.
doc_datepicker_guideline_1: Use when users need to select a specific date or ranges with visual calendar confirmation.
doc_datepicker_guideline_2: For dates users know by heart (e.g., birth dates, expiry dates), direct text input is often more efficient.
doc_datepicker_integration_desc: DatePicker utilizes the shared Calendar component internally. See the Calendar documentation for more details.
doc_datepicker_kb_1: Press Enter or Space on the input field to open the calendar.
doc_datepicker_kb_2: Navigate through dates with arrow keys and press Enter to select.
doc_datepicker_kb_3: Close the calendar anytime by pressing the Escape key.
doc_datepicker_long_desc: Details and usage for the Date Picker component.
doc_datepicker_design_intent: DatePicker is a component designed to allow users to intuitively select dates through a calendar-format UI. By being able to see the sense of the day of the week and specific periods (yesterday, tomorrow, etc.) while selecting, it prevents entry errors and reduces the user's memory load.
doc_datepicker_choice_matrix_desc: Select the optimal entry method depending on the nature of the date to be entered.
doc_datepicker_choice_picker_when: When it's necessary to select while checking the day of the week or availability (e.g., reservation date, event date).
doc_datepicker_choice_input_when: When entering a date the user already remembers quickly (e.g., date of birth, expiration date).
doc_datepicker_anatomy_input: Input: A field that displays the currently selected date and accepts keyboard input.
doc_datepicker_anatomy_popup: Calendar Popup: A floating panel for visually listing and selecting dates.
doc_datepicker_content_format: Display the appropriate date format (YYYY/MM/DD, etc.) for the user's locale and communicate entry examples through placeholders.
doc_datepicker_motion_fade: Add light fade and slide effects when the popup appears to clarify where the calendar is appearing from.
doc_datepicker_a11y_keyboard: Navigate through dates in the calendar with arrow keys and make selection possible with the Enter key. Also, allow closing the calendar with the Escape key.
doc_datepicker_scenario_reserve: Reservation System: Selecting check-in or visit dates.
doc_datepicker_ts_desc: The DatePicker component provides comprehensive type definitions for safe usage.
doc_daterangepicker_long_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the daterangepicker component.
doc_daterangepicker_title: A component for selecting a specific date range (start and end dates).
doc_dropzone_accept_desc: You can use the accept property to limit the file formats that can be uploaded.
doc_dropzone_long_desc: A component that supports file uploads via drag and drop.
doc_dropzone_multiple_desc: By setting the multiple property to true, you can select and drop multiple files.
doc_dropzone_title: Area for uploading files via drag and drop
doc_dropzone_design_intent: Dropzone is a component designed to allow users to intuitively upload files. It improves the speed and experience of the operation by providing a 'large container' where users can drag and drop directly from the desktop, saving the trouble of searching through a file selection dialog.
doc_dropzone_anatomy_area: Drop Area: A large area surrounded by a border for dropping files into.
doc_dropzone_anatomy_text: Guide Text: Instructional messages such as 'Drop files here'.
doc_dropzone_content_limit: Clearly state the permitted file formats and maximum size so that users can make a judgment before uploading.
doc_dropzone_motion_active: The background color or border changes when a file enters the area (Drag Over), providing visual feedback that it's ready for acceptance.
doc_dropzone_a11y_fallback: Always provide an alternative means of opening a file browser by clicking for users who have difficulty with drag and drop.
doc_dropzone_scenario_docs: Document Submission: Submitting PDF or Office files.
doc_fieldLabelContent_desc: Label text combined with required/optional badge
doc_fieldLabelContent_long_desc: FieldLabelContent renders the label text along with optional required or optional status badges.
doc_fieldLabelContent_summary: Component for displaying label text and required/optional badges
doc_fieldLabelContent_usage_desc: Used inside FieldTemplate to render the label area with required/optional indicators.
doc_fieldTemplate_desc: Common layout (label, error) for form fields
doc_fieldTemplate_long_desc: FieldTemplate provides the standard layout wrapper for form fields, combining the label, optional/required badge, input slot, and error message in a consistent structure.
doc_fieldTemplate_summary: Common layout for form fields (labels, error display)
doc_fieldTemplate_usage_desc: Wraps any form input to provide consistent label positioning, error display, and accessibility association.
doc_fielderror_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation used to display validation error messages below input fields.
doc_fielderror_long_desc: Details and usage for the Field Error component.
doc_fieldset_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation for grouping related form elements with a legend.
doc_fieldset_disabled_desc: Disables all nested form elements at once using the disabled property.
doc_fieldset_long_desc: Details and usage for the Fieldset component.
doc_fieldset_title: A container used to group related elements in a form, often with a legend.
doc_fieldset_with_legend_desc: Use the Fieldset.Legend subcomponent to provide a clear title for the group.
doc_fileupload_accept_desc: You can limit the types of files (MIME types, extensions) that can be selected using the `accept` property.
doc_fileupload_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation allowing users to select or drag and drop files.
doc_fileupload_long_desc: Details and usage for the File Upload component.
doc_fileupload_multi_file_desc: Enable multiple file selection by setting the multiple property.
doc_fileupload_multiple_desc: Setting the `multiple` property allows selecting multiple files simultaneously.
doc_fileupload_restricted_desc: Limit selectable files to specific formats (e.g., images only) using the accept property.
doc_fileupload_usage_desc: Files can be uploaded by dragging & dropping or clicking to select.
doc_fileupload_with_icon_desc: An example of a customized upload area containing an icon.
doc_indicatorSlider_desc: Sliding highlight indicator for Tabs, SegmentedControl, etc.
doc_indicatorSlider_long_desc: IndicatorSlider is an animated sliding highlight component that tracks an active element and smoothly moves to indicate the current selection.
doc_indicatorSlider_summary: Sliding indicator for Tabs, SegmentedControl, etc.
doc_indicatorSlider_usage_desc: Used internally by Tabs and SegmentedControl to display an animated active indicator.
doc_inputBase_desc: Shared shell wrapper for Input, Selectbox, etc. (icons, clear button)
doc_inputBase_long_desc: InputBase is the shared shell wrapper for input components. It provides a consistent container with optional icons, status styles (error, disabled), and action buttons (clear).
doc_inputBase_summary: Shared shell component for Input, Selectbox, etc. (icons, clear button)
doc_inputBase_usage_desc: Used internally by Input, Selectbox, Combobox, SearchInput and others to ensure a consistent visual and functional shell.
doc_input_basic_desc: Standard implementation of the text input field for user data entry.
doc_input_clearable_desc: Example displaying a button to clear input content.
doc_input_desc: Single-line input fields for text, numbers, or emails
doc_input_ghost_desc: A simple style without a background color. It doesn’t emphasize the field normally, providing visual feedback only on hover or focus.
doc_input_ghost_i1: Empty: Placeholder suggests it is an input area
doc_input_ghost_i2: Hover: Displays a light background to notify it is intractable
doc_input_ghost_i3: Focus: Displays a primary-colored border to clearly show input state
doc_input_ghost_u1: Click-to-Edit UIs for titles on detail screens where display is primary
doc_input_ghost_u2: Noise reduction in screens with many input items like lists or tables
doc_input_ghost_u3: When you want to blend the search bar into the background
doc_input_long_desc: Details and usage for the Input component.
doc_input_outline_desc: Standard style with a border (same as default, but used when specifying explicitly).
doc_searchinput_design_intent: SearchInput is an input component specialized for searching information. It comes standard with a search icon that communicates 'searching purpose' at a glance and a clear button to quickly discard complex search terms, optimizing the user's discovery experience.
doc_searchinput_anatomy_icon: Search Icon: Placed on the left, suggesting that the purpose of the field is 'search'.
doc_searchinput_anatomy_clear: Clear Button: Displayed on the right when there is input, allowing content to be erased with one click.
doc_searchinput_content_placeholder: Set text that specifically indicates the search target, such as 'Enter keyword' or 'Search server'.
doc_searchinput_motion_clear: A fade effect is applied to the appearance and disappearance of the clear button to communicate UI changes naturally.
doc_searchinput_a11y_label: Even with a visual icon, always set an accessibility label (aria-label="Search") to clearly state the purpose.
doc_searchinput_scenario_global: Global Search: Site-wide search placed in the header.
doc_searchinput_scenario_list: List Filter: Search placed at the top of a table or list for item extraction.
doc_input_title: A standard field for users to enter short text freely
doc_input_design_intent: Inputs (text fields) are the most basic components for obtaining short, free-form text information from users, such as names, addresses, and email addresses.
doc_input_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on the amount and nature of the information to be entered.
doc_input_choice_textarea_when: Suitable for long, multi-line text or comments.
doc_input_choice_number_when: Suitable for numeric-only input (including increment/decrement operations) such as quantities or amounts.
doc_input_anatomy_container: Container: Forms the border and background, clarifying the input area.
doc_input_anatomy_field: Input Field: The <input> element where the user actually types text.
doc_input_anatomy_icons: Icon Slots: Places for search icons, clear buttons, etc. (Prefix/Suffix).
doc_input_content_labels: Keep labels short and clear, prioritizing item format like 'Name' over question format.
doc_input_content_placeholder: Use placeholders for input examples (e.g., John Doe) and do not use them as a replacement for labels.
doc_input_motion_focus: The border color changes smoothly (transition) on focus to indicate that it is active.
doc_input_a11y_labels: Link a <label> to every input field and do not rely solely on placeholders.
doc_input_a11y_aria: When an error occurs, use aria-invalid="true" and aria-describedby to explicitly link it with the error message.
doc_input_scenario_form: Contact Form: Entering various attribute information.
doc_input_scenario_search: Search Bar: Entering single keywords for site-wide search.
doc_input_variant_desc: You can change the style with the `variant` property.
doc_input_width_desc: By setting the `width` according to the length of the input content, you can visually convey to the user "what should be entered" (affordance). WIM UI provides semantic sizes (recommended) from `xs` to `xl`, which are responsive (automatically shrink to full width on smartphones).
doc_input_width_note: * Note that the `ch` unit is based on the width of the half-width digit "0", so when entering full-width characters (Japanese) or wide characters, the number of displayed characters may be fewer than expected. For multi-language support or general form design, the use of semantic sizes (`sm`, `md`, etc.) is recommended.
doc_input_width_usage: For detailed design guidelines, please also refer to the [Form UI Guide](/docs/guides-form-ui--docs#appropriate-width-setting-for-input-fields).
doc_inputgroup_basic_desc: Example adding a prefix to an Input using InputGroupText.
doc_inputgroup_fullwidth_desc: Using the fullWidth property expands it to the container width.
doc_inputgroup_long_desc: The InputGroup component is used to visually unify an Input with buttons or text (addons).
doc_inputgroup_multi_desc: Multiple InputGroupText components can be lined up.
doc_inputgroup_note: InputGroupText is for displaying information like units or prefixes. If clickable actions are needed, combine with the Button component.
doc_inputgroup_suffix_desc: Text addons can be added after the Input.
doc_inputgroup_with_button_desc: Action buttons can be placed next to the Input.
doc_inputmask_basic_desc: Specify a pattern in the mask property.
doc_inputmask_long_desc: An input component with a mask to enforce input following a specific format.
doc_label_desc: Descriptive text for input fields, including required/optional marks
doc_label_long_desc: A component for displaying descriptions or captions for form elements.
doc_label_req_opt_desc: Use the equired property for a required mark and showOptional for an optional mark.
doc_label_title: Descriptive text for input fields, including required/optional marks
doc_legend_long_desc: The Legend component is used to display a group title within a Fieldset.
doc_multiselect_long_desc: A dropdown menu for users to select multiple options from a predefined list.
doc_multiselect_design_intent: MultiSelect is a component for users to efficiently select multiple items from a large number of choices. Selected items are displayed as 'tags' within the field, designed to allow easy verification and deletion of content.
doc_multiselect_choice_matrix_desc: Select according to the number of choices and the priority of the operation.
doc_multiselect_choice_multi_when: When there are many choices (10 or more) and you want to display the selected content compactly.
doc_multiselect_choice_checkbox_when: When there are few choices (around 5) and you want all choices to be verifiable at a glance.
doc_multiselect_anatomy_tags: Selected Tags: Display selected items in a chip format. Can be deleted individually with an X mark.
doc_multiselect_content_search: By enabling the search function, you can find the desired item quickly from a large list.
doc_multiselect_motion_tag: When an item is selected, a tag appears softly, visually connecting that it has moved from the list to the field.
doc_multiselect_a11y_announcement: Notify screen readers of the current selection status when items are added or deleted.
doc_multiselect_scenario_skill: User Settings: Selecting skills or categories of interest.
doc_multiselect_scenario_admin: Permission Management: Assigning multiple roles to a user.
doc_numberinput_long_desc: A component that provides numerical input.
doc_numberinput_title: Field specialized for numeric input with spin buttons
doc_numberinput_design_intent: NumberInput is a component specialized for entering and fine-tuning numerical values. In addition to users directly entering numbers using the keyboard, it provides a UI where values can be intuitively adjusted using spin buttons (increment/decrement buttons).
doc_numberinput_choice_matrix_desc: Select according to the nature of the value to be entered and the frequency of adjustment.
doc_numberinput_choice_number_when: When continuous increment/decrement operations of numerical values such as quantity, age, or price are expected.
doc_numberinput_choice_text_when: For 'numbers' with a strong symbolic meaning, such as phone numbers, credit card numbers, or zip codes (see [Numeric Input Standardization](/docs/guides-numeric-input-standardization--docs)).
doc_numberinput_choice_slider_when: When intuitive proportions or sensory adjustments within a range are prioritized over precise numerical values.
doc_numberinput_anatomy_input: Input: An <input> element that accepts only numerical values.
doc_numberinput_anatomy_steppers: Steppers: Up and down buttons for increasing or decreasing the value by a certain amount.
doc_numberinput_content_precision: When decimal precision is important, such as for currency or weight, make appropriate settings and communicate expected values to the user.
doc_numberinput_motion_spin: Numerical values update smoothly when buttons are pressed, communicating the certainty of the operation.
doc_numberinput_a11y_stepper: Assign appropriate aria-labels to the spin buttons so their function can be understood by screen readers.
doc_numberinput_scenario_cart: Shopping Cart: Changing the number of items ordered (quantity).
doc_numberinput_scenario_admin: Admin Panel: Specifying the sort order (priority) numerically.
doc_otpinput_length_change_desc: Change the number of input fields with the length property.
doc_otpinput_long_desc: Split input fields for entering one-time passwords (OTP) or PIN codes.
doc_otpinput_design_intent: OtpInput is a specialized component for entering short authentication codes (usually 4 to 6 digits) used in two-factor authentication, etc. By providing fields categorized for each character, it clarifies the current entry position and reduces entry errors by functioning as a visual guide.
doc_otpinput_anatomy_fields: Input Field Sequence: A group of inputs dedicated to a single character, lined up for the specified number of digits.
doc_otpinput_content_auto: To enhance convenience on mobile devices, it supports automatic completion from SMS (autocomplete="one-time-code").
doc_otpinput_motion_focus: Provides a pleasant transition where focus automatically moves to the next field (autofocus) with each character entered.
doc_otpinput_a11y_label: Communicate to screen readers that each field is requesting individual entry, and make them recognize it as an 'authentication code entry' as a whole group.
doc_otpinput_scenario_auth: Identity Verification: Entering codes during login or password reset.
doc_otpinput_scenario_pay: Payment Confirmation: Entering security codes during high-value payments.
doc_passwordinput_design_intent: PasswordInput is a component for securely entering sensitive information such as passwords. In addition to the function of masking the entered content, it provides a 'show/hide' toggle function so that users can confirm whether they have entered it correctly, reducing stress caused by entry errors.
doc_passwordinput_anatomy_visibility: Visibility Toggle Button: Toggles between masking (●●●) the input content and displaying it as plain text.
doc_passwordinput_content_security: For security reasons, always start with 'hidden (masking)' by default.
doc_passwordinput_a11y_toggle: Explicitly state the state of the visibility toggle button (show/hide) using aria-label so that the current mode can be recognized by assistive technologies.
doc_passwordinput_scenario_account: Account Settings: Confirming current passwords or setting new ones.
doc_passwordinput_long_desc: Enhanced input field specifically for secure password entry with visibility toggle.
doc_radiogroup_title: Management component for grouping related radio buttons
doc_radiogroup_design_intent: RadioGroup is a component for grouping multiple exclusive choices (only one can be selected) and managing a single selection state. It visually and structurally clarifies the constraint that the user must select exactly one.
doc_radiogroup_choice_matrix_desc: Select the optimal component according to the number of choices and the importance of the operation.
doc_radiogroup_choice_radio_when: When there are about 3 to 5 choices and you want to keep all choices exposed at all times.
doc_radiogroup_choice_selectbox_when: When there are 5 or more choices and you want to save vertical space.
doc_radiogroup_choice_segmented_when: When you want to operate with a feeling closer to control buttons, such as switching ON/OFF or switching views.
doc_radiogroup_anatomy_wrapper: Wrapper: A container that groups radio buttons. Can be arranged vertically or horizontally.
doc_radiogroup_content_default: To clarify the user's intent, usually select one of the items by default or specify that it is a required field.
doc_radiogroup_motion_focus: Visually emphasize the currently focused item during tab key navigation to guide keyboard operations.
doc_radiogroup_a11y_group: Use role="radiogroup" and ensure standard behavior where items can be navigated between using keyboard arrow keys.
doc_radiogroup_scenario_gender: Gender Selection: Single selection from limited choices.
doc_radiogroup_scenario_plan: Price Plan: Selecting one from Plan A, B, or C.
doc_radio_long_desc: Radio buttons allow users to select one option from a set.
doc_radio_title: A control that allows users to choose only one from a set of mutually exclusive options
doc_radio_design_intent: Radio buttons are used to force a single selection from multiple mutually exclusive choices.
doc_radio_choice_matrix_desc: Decide based on the number of choices and whether the user needs to see all options.
doc_radio_choice_radio_when: When there are few choices (5 or fewer) and you want a bird's-eye view of all items.
doc_radio_choice_select_when: When there are many choices (6 or more) and you want to prioritize space saving.
doc_radio_anatomy_button: Indicator: The center dot indicating the selection state, mimicking traditional physical buttons.
doc_radio_anatomy_label: Label: The item name. Selectable by clicking the text as well.
doc_radio_content_consistency: Type labels consistently across all items to make them easier to compare.
doc_radio_motion_pop: Add a subtle scale animation when the center dot appears upon selection to enhance responsiveness.
doc_radio_a11y_keyboard: Move between items using arrow keys; selection changes automatically.
doc_radio_a11y_group: Wrap in role="radiogroup" to tell assistive technologies that the choices are related.
doc_radio_scenario_payment: Payment Method Selection: Selecting one from credit card, bank transfer, etc.
doc_radio_scenario_plan: Plan Selection: Upgrading or downgrading a service plan.
doc_radio_note_default: It is recommended to set a default value for the most likely choice (e.g., Standard Shipping).
doc_radio_note_layout: Stack options vertically and ensure labels are clickable to improve usability.
doc_radio_when_to_use: Best for selecting exactly one option from 3 or more choices. Consider Switch for 2 states (On/Off) or Checkbox for multiple selections.
doc_radiogroup_long_desc: RadioGroup manages a set of Radio buttons.
doc_checkboxgroup_title: Management component for grouping multiple checkboxes
doc_checkboxgroup_design_intent: CheckboxGroup is a component for organizing related multiple checkboxes as a logical group and managing a common state (an array of values). It provides a consistent layout and operability in scenarios where users need to select multiple options at once.
doc_checkboxgroup_anatomy_label: Group Label: Explicitly states what the group is for selecting (e.g., 'Fields of Interest').
doc_checkboxgroup_anatomy_items: Checkbox Items: Individual options lined up within the group.
doc_checkboxgroup_content_sort: If there are many choices, list them in an order that is easy for users to find, such as alphabetical or by frequency of use.
doc_checkboxgroup_motion_stagger: When lists are displayed dynamically, items appear sequentially and softly to visually communicate the grouping of information.
doc_checkboxgroup_a11y_fieldset: Internally use <fieldset> and <legend> (or set appropriate WAI-ARIA attributes) to communicate to assistive technologies that these are a group of answers to a single question.
doc_checkboxgroup_scenario_hobby: User Profile: Multiple selection of hobbies or special skills.
doc_checkboxgroup_scenario_filter: Search Filter: Specifying multiple product categories or brands.
doc_rangeslider_long_desc: A slider component for selecting a range (start and end).
doc_rating_half_desc: Using the allowHalf prop allows for ratings in increments of 0.5.
doc_rating_long_desc: A component for displaying and entering ratings.
doc_rating_disabled_desc: Using the disabled prop makes the component non-interactive and grays it out.
doc_rating_readonly_desc: Using the readOnly prop makes the component display-only. Unlike disabled, the stars retain their normal color — ideal for showing other users' ratings.
doc_rating_size_desc: Supports three sizes: small, medium, and large.
doc_rating_design_intent: Rating is a component for users to feedback 'sensory satisfaction' such as a 5-star rating for products or services. It provides a visual UI that is more intuitive than numeric input, allowing evaluation content to be understood at a glance.
doc_rating_anatomy_stars: Star Elements: Icons (usually star-shaped) indicating the current rating level.
doc_rating_content_precision: Set appropriately whether to allow input in 0.5 increments (half-stars) depending on the granularity of the evaluation.
doc_rating_motion_hover: Enhance operational responsiveness with a preview effect (hover feedback) where the star color changes on mouseover.
doc_rating_a11y_reading: Clearly communicate the current value (e.g., 'Current 4.5 stars') and the maximum value (e.g., 'out of 5 stars') to screen readers.
doc_rating_scenario_review: Product Review: Evaluating satisfaction of purchased items with stars.
doc_rating_scenario_survey: User Survey: Answering the ease of use of a service on a 5-point scale.
doc_searchinput_long_desc: A component that provides a search interface.
doc_searchinput_title: A text input field specifically designed for search queries, including a search icon.
doc_selectbox_a11y_1: Proper use of role="combobox" and role="listbox"
doc_selectbox_a11y_2: State management for aria-expanded, aria-selected, and aria-disabled
doc_selectbox_a11y_desc: The Selectbox component complies with WAI-ARIA accessibility guidelines:
doc_selectbox_adv_comb_desc: Search and grouping can be used simultaneously. Only groups containing options that match the search criteria will be displayed.
doc_selectbox_adv_search_desc: Allows users to search through options. Useful when there are many choices.
doc_selectbox_best_practice_1: If there are more than 10 options, enable Searchable Select functionality to reduce the user's search burden.
doc_selectbox_best_practice_2: If there are many related items, use Grouped options to organize them.
doc_selectbox_custom_filter_desc: By default, a case-insensitive partial match search is performed, but you can also provide a custom filter function.
doc_selectbox_desc: A dropdown menu for selecting one option from a predefined list
doc_selectbox_guideline_1: Use when there are many options and space is insufficient for RadioGroups or SegmentedControls.
doc_selectbox_guideline_2: If there are 5 or fewer options, consider using highly visible RadioButtons or SegmentedControls.
doc_selectbox_long_desc: A dropdown menu for users to select one option from a predefined set.
doc_selectbox_op_first: Focus first option with Home key
doc_selectbox_op_last: Focus last option with End key
doc_selectbox_op_next: Focus next option with down arrow key
doc_selectbox_op_prev: Focus previous option with up arrow key
doc_selectbox_title: A dropdown menu for selecting one option from a predefined list
doc_selectbox_design_intent: Selectboxes are components used to accurately select one option from a large number of choices within a limited display space. By hiding choices in the default state, they reduce screen complexity.
doc_selectbox_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate input format based on the number of choices and screen space.
doc_selectbox_choice_select_when: When there are 6 or more options, or when you want to save space.
doc_selectbox_choice_radio_when: When there are few options (5 or fewer) and you want to show all options at once.
doc_selectbox_choice_combobox_when: When there are a very large number of options and the user wants to narrow them down by free entry or search.
doc_selectbox_anatomy_trigger: Trigger: The area that displays the current value and expands the menu on click.
doc_selectbox_anatomy_menu: Menu: The floating area that displays the options (Options).
doc_selectbox_anatomy_search: Search Field: The input field for finding items among a large number of choices.
doc_selectbox_content_placeholder: Set text like 'Select...' that prompts the user for the next action.
doc_selectbox_content_labels: Keep labels in the list concise and ideally fitting on a single line.
doc_selectbox_motion_opacity: Smoothly displays the menu with a combination of fade and subtle slide when expanded.
doc_selectbox_a11y_listbox: Uses role='listbox' and indicates the currently selected item with aria-selected.
doc_selectbox_a11y_scroll: Automatically scrolls so that the focused item is always within the display area during keyboard operation.
doc_selectbox_scenario_country: Country Selection: For 200+ options, a selectbox with search functionality is ideal.
doc_selectbox_scenario_form: Profile Settings: A space-saving selection method when there are many items on the screen.
doc_selectbox_ts_desc: The Selectbox component provides full type definitions.
doc_selectbox_var_native_desc: Enables the browser-standard `<select>` element. In modern browsers (e.g., Chrome 119+), separators are supported within native selects using `<hr>`. Options with `type: "separator"` will be rendered as `<hr>`.
doc_selectbox_var_sep_desc: A dividing line can be displayed between options by specifying `type: "separator"`.
doc_signaturepad_long_desc: SignaturePad is a component for entering handwritten signatures via mouse or touch.
doc_signaturepad_var_color_desc: You can change the color and thickness of the pen (signature).
doc_slider_basic_desc: Standard implementation for selecting a single numeric value from a range.
doc_slider_custom_marks_desc: Labels and tick marks can be displayed at specific values using the `marks` property.
doc_slider_desc: An input to capture a numeric value from a specified range by dragging a knob
doc_slider_long_desc: Details and usage for the Slider component.
doc_slider_range_desc: Use multiple knobs to specify a range of start and end values.
doc_slider_step_desc: Restrict the numeric increment (step size) using the `step` property.
doc_slider_title: Input to capture a numeric value from a specified range by dragging a knob
doc_slider_design_intent: Sliders are components for selecting a numerical value between a minimum and maximum value through intuitive drag operations. They are ideal when 'relative position' within a range or 'sensory adjustment' is prioritized over precise numerical input.
doc_slider_choice_matrix_desc: Select according to the nature of the value being selected and the operational precision expected by the user.
doc_slider_choice_slider_when: When sensory adjustment is more suitable than strict numerical values, such as volume, brightness, or price range.
doc_slider_choice_number_when: When you want to specify a concrete numerical value precisely in single units, such as age or quantity.
doc_slider_anatomy_track: Track: A line indicating the range of value movement. Colors are separated between selected and unselected ranges.
doc_slider_anatomy_thumb: Knob (Thumb): A circular handle that the user drags to change the value.
doc_slider_anatomy_marks: Marks: Visually guide key numerical points.
doc_slider_content_labels: Display labels for the minimum and maximum values to clarify what is being adjusted.
doc_slider_motion_slide: The track color changes smoothly following the movement of the knob, providing dynamic feedback of the current value.
doc_slider_a11y_keyboard: Ensure values can be increased or decreased by fixed amounts using the left/right (up/down) arrow keys.
doc_slider_scenario_filter: Price Filter: Narrowing down a specific price range from a product list (RangeSlider).
doc_slider_usage_desc: Select a numeric value intuitively from the set range by dragging the knob.
doc_slider_vertical_desc: Align the slider vertically using the `vertical` property.
doc_switch_title: Instantly toggle a single setting on or off
doc_switch_desc: A binary switch for instantly toggling settings between on and off states
doc_switch_long_desc: Switches are used to toggle the state of a single setting on or off.
doc_switch_design_intent: A Switch is used when the user needs to toggle a single setting that takes effect immediately. Unlike a Checkbox, a Switch implies immediate action without requiring a submit button.
doc_switch_choice_matrix_desc: Choose the most appropriate component based on when the change takes effect.
doc_switch_choice_switch_when: When toggling a feature immediately changes the application state (e.g. dark mode, notifications).
doc_switch_choice_check_when: When the change takes effect only after a form submission or confirmation.
doc_switch_anatomy_track: Track: The background element that visually indicates the on/off state via color change.
doc_switch_anatomy_thumb: Thumb: The circular handle that slides along the track to reflect the current state.
doc_switch_content_labels: Accompany the switch with a label that describes what will happen when the state changes (e.g. Receive notifications).
doc_switch_motion_slide: The thumb slides to reproduce the physical feel of a real switch, emphasizing the tactile feedback of toggling.
doc_switch_a11y_keyboard: The Space key can be used to toggle on/off.
doc_switch_a11y_role: Uses role="switch" to inform screen readers that this is a switch control.
doc_switch_scenario_setting: Settings Screen: Toggling notification settings, dark mode, etc.
doc_switch_scenario_filtering: Filtering: Toggling specific conditions on/off (e.g. In-stock items only).
doc_switch_scenario_settings: Settings Screen: Toggling notification settings or privacy settings.
doc_switchgroup_design_intent: SwitchGroup is a component for managing multiple related toggle switches collectively. It is suitable for control panels where users need to turn multiple optional features or types of notifications on or off.
doc_switchgroup_anatomy_label: Group Label: A title that indicates the overall category of settings.
doc_switchgroup_anatomy_items: Switch Items: Individual settings consisting of a toggle switch and a descriptive label.
doc_switchgroup_content_immediate: Since switches often imply immediate reflection, ensure that the UI clearly communicates that each setting has been updated.
doc_switchgroup_motion_active: When a switch is toggled, visual feedback is provided immediately through color and position changes.
doc_switchgroup_a11y_list: Groups switches together using list markup or role="group" to clarify their logical relationship to screen readers.
doc_switchgroup_scenario_notify: Communication Settings: Toggling different types of notifications (Email, SMS, Push).
doc_switchgroup_scenario_privacy: Privacy Control: Toggling different data collection permissions.
doc_switchgroup_long_desc: A group of switch components used to handle multiple toggle selections.
doc_timepicker_desc: A component for users to select time. It wraps the native <input type="time"> and adjusts the styling.
doc_timepicker_title: A specialized input for selecting specific hours and minutes
doc_treeselect_long_desc: TreeSelect is a component for selecting one or more items from a tree-like list.
doc_treeselect_multi_desc: In multi-selection mode, you can select multiple nodes using checkboxes.
doc_advanced_input_desc: Used for special input formats such as file uploads, complex selections, and ratings.
guide_numeric_std_desc: Explains the problems with input type="number" and guidelines for proper numeric input design.
guide_numeric_std_concept_desc: Do not use type="number" just because the input is numeric; select the most appropriate attribute based on data characteristics.
guide_numeric_std_usage_warning_desc: Suitable for incrementable values (quantities, etc.), but not for 'symbolic numbers' like credit card or zip codes.
guide_numeric_std_exp_notation_desc: Large numbers can result in exponential notation like 'e+26', making spin buttons dysfunctional.
guide_numeric_std_invalid_chars_desc: Characters like '+', '-', '.', and 'e' can be entered, so relying solely on validation is risky.
guide_numeric_std_problem_1_desc: Safari and Firefox allow alphabet input, which is handled as an empty string in JavaScript.
guide_numeric_std_problem_2_desc: Full-width numbers may be treated as empty strings or automatically converted depending on the browser.
guide_numeric_std_problem_3_desc: Layout may break when font size is changed, requiring specific CSS hacks.
guide_numeric_std_problem_4_desc: Spin buttons can strip leading zeros (e.g., '0123' becomes '124'), making it unsuitable for IDs or phone numbers.
guide_numeric_std_recommend_desc: For identification numbers, the combination of type="text" and inputmode="numeric" is recommended.
guide_numeric_std_table_input_js_empty: Allowed (Treated as empty string in JS)
guide_numeric_std_safari_behavior: Treated as an empty string if included, similar to alphabets
guide_numeric_std_spin_button_warning: Apply the following styles to prevent unintended value changes via spin buttons, or consider using `type="text"`.
guide_numeric_std_recommend_cc_example: Recommended format for credit cards, etc.
